Category: Agriculture & Resources: Agriculture, Forestry, Fishing, Mining, Energy
Who is this for: Operations managers, farm managers, site supervisors, field technicians, production coordinators, safety officers, data analysts, agricultural specialists, mining engineers, forestry supervisors, energy facility operators
Team or Dept this is for: Operations, Production, Safety, Risk Management, Agricultural Operations, Mining Operations, Energy Operations, Forestry Management
Description: How inaccurate AI insights can lead to dangerous operational decisions.
Prerequisites: A basic familiarity with day-to-day operations in at least one agricultural, forestry, fishing, mining, or energy sector is helpful, along with a general curiosity about how digital tools and data-driven systems are being used in those industries.
Learning objectives: By the end of this course, you will be able to identify the key warning signs of overconfident AI predictions and explain how misleading outputs can lead to harmful decisions in agricultural, forestry, and resource-based operations. You will also apply practical strategies to critically evaluate AI-generated insights, helping you protect your organisation from costly and potentially dangerous mistakes in the field.
